participants.name: Chris Johnson
participants.notes: Did not run 40 at pro day. Limited workout — did shuttle and positional drills only per team/scout request. Had standout NFL Combine performance (4.40 forty, 38.0" vertical, 126" broad, 17 bench reps, 6.84 three-cone). Combine arms 30 5/8", hands 9 7/8". Projected second-round pick. Private workouts with Dallas, Minnesota, L.A. Rams, and New Orleans.

participants.name: Bryce Phillips
participants.notes: Impressed scouts with measurables and speed. Brother of Atlanta Falcons CB Clark Phillips III. Transferred from Tennessee State.

participants.name: Eric Butler
participants.notes: Has private session scheduled with Dallas Cowboys.

participants.name: Deshawn McCuin
participants.notes: Fastest 40 time among all pro day participants. Formerly at TCU. Played STUD/nickel position at SDSU.

participants.name: Sam Benjamin
participants.notes: Senior defensive lineman. No specific drill results reported.

participants.name: Niles King
participants.notes: 6-6, 260 per 247Sports. Transfer from Grand Valley State (Division II). Had 6.5 sacks in 2025 season. No specific drill results reported.

participants.name: DJ Herman
participants.notes: Special teams standout. Said agility/shuttle was his strongest drill. Career-long SDSU player. No specific drill results reported.

participants.name: Mister Williams
participants.notes: Transfer from Incarnate Word. No specific drill results reported.

participants.name: Christian Jones
participants.notes: Listed 6-9 by 247Sports; weight ~330 per school sources. Career-long SDSU player. Left tackle. Regarded as late-round draft candidate. No specific drill results reported.

participants.name: Bayo Kannike
participants.notes: Transfer from Utah Tech. All-Mountain West Honorable Mention in 2025. No specific drill results reported.

participants.name: Ross Ulugalu-Maseuli
participants.notes: Center. First-Team All-Mountain West in 2025. Did not run the 40-yard dash at pro day. Participated in measurements and positional drills. Career-long SDSU player from Mater Dei HS.

participants.name: Seth Adams
participants.notes: 6-2, 260 per GoAztecs roster. Transfer from SE Louisiana (originally Kennesaw State). All-Mountain West Honorable Mention in 2025. Converted from offensive lineman to tight end. No specific drill results reported.

participants.name: Gabriel Plascencia
participants.notes: Kicking workout only — did not participate in athletic testing drills. Made 11 of 14 field goal attempts including makes of 61 and 64 yards. Missed from 65 yards (wide left). Most accurate kicker in SDSU history (36-for-41, .878 career FG%). Set school record with 25 consecutive made FGs.

participants.name: Malachi Cooper
participants.notes: Visiting prospect from University of San Diego (USD). 6-4, 280 per SD Union-Tribune. Three-time All-Pioneer Football League selection. Had 5 sacks and 41 tackles in 2025 final season. Played inside and outside for the Toreros. Scouts view him as developmental D-line prospect who could contribute on special teams.

participants.name: Matt Colombo
participants.notes: Visiting prospect from University of San Diego (USD). Originally at USC (2020-2023). Finished 2025 season rushing for four TDs vs Stetson in final game.

participants.name: Mo Vanderwiel
participants.notes: Visiting prospect from University of San Diego (USD). Previously at Wagner College. Had an interception returned for a TD in final game of career vs Stetson.

additional_notes: Pro Day held Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at SDSU's Fowler Athletic Center (indoor measurements/testing) and outdoor practice fields. Scouts from 30 of the NFL's 32 teams attended. 13 SDSU players participated alongside 3 visiting players from the University of San Diego. Players were measured for height, weight, hand and wingspan indoors, then tested on bench press, vertical jump and broad jump before heading outside for the 40-yard dash, shuttle, three-cone, and positional drills. All but three SDSU players (Johnson, Plascencia, Ulugalu-Maseuli) ran the 40-yard dash. Official individual times were largely not released to media. The reported 40 times for Phillips (4.56), Butler (4.58), and McCuin (4.52) came from the San Diego Union-Tribune and East Village Times beat reporters; timing method (electronic vs. hand) was not specified. Note: Some sources (Daily Aztec photo captions) reference the date as March 11 (Wednesday), but the SD Union-Tribune preview and results articles confirm the event was scheduled and held on Tuesday, March 10. Chris Johnson (CB) was the headliner as a projected Day 2 pick but only did a limited workout after his strong NFL Combine showing.
